program: Business English — Executive Communication for International Leadership
hub_subtitle: Business English Executivo para Reuniões Internacionais e Liderança

lesson.menu_desc: Interpreting an ambiguous business situation -- modals of deduction (must / might / could / can't + be and + have + participle) -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Reading the Signals -- Interpreting What Must Have Happened
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Izabel Cristina de Pieri | Aula 11 -- Reading the Signals

listenings.text: Hi Izabel, it is Ethan, your account manager, thinking out loud about the client. Two weeks of silence after such a strong meeting. Honestly, I am trying not to jump to conclusions, but let me read the signals with you. First, they must be busy, not gone. Their procurement lead was traveling, so the delay could just be a scheduling thing. That is the plausible read. But there is one red flag. A colleague of mine saw their team at a supplier fair last week, so they might be talking to a rival to compare prices. Now, they cannot have signed with anyone yet, because our quote is still open and they asked us to hold it. My gut feeling? They are stalling to gain a little leverage. If I read between the lines of that last email, the tone was warmer than the silence suggests. So I would not speculate too far. I would rule out the worst case and simply call them Thursday. They must want the deal. They just want it on better terms. Talk soon.

Good morning, Izabel. It is Laura, your market analyst, with a quick read on the competitor. That sudden price cut yesterday surprised everyone, so let me help you interpret it rather than react. First, they cannot be making money at that number. It is below cost, which means the cut must be a short-term move to win one big account, not a new strategy. My assumption is simple. They must have lost a major client last quarter, and this looks like a defensive play. It might be a signal that their pipeline is thin. Now, some people will say we should match them, but I would rule that out. If we cut too, our margin would vanish, and honestly that could be exactly what they want. Do not read too much into one price, and do not hold back your own story. Your quality and lead time still win the serious buyers. My gut feeling is that this cut is far-fetched as a long-term threat. Read the signal for what it is, a bluff, and stay the course. Call me if you want to talk it through.
